title: Exact Finite Order And Lattice Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite order antisymmetry QF_UF/Alethe regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Antisymmetry conflict where x <= y and y <= x force x = y, but the malformed row also asserts x != y.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional finite order or lattice rows only when they add distinct equality, monotonicity, or table solver pressure; keep complete lattices and fixed-point theorems in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: Encode fixed finite order and lattice tables as deterministic finite-relation and finite-function obligations.
Replay poset, meet, join, distributivity, monotone-map, and fixed-point witnesses through Axeyum model evaluation.
Route bad finite antisymmetry conflicts through checked QF_UF/Alethe evidence once exact relation replay identifies the failing pair.
Keep general lattice theory, complete lattices, fixed-point theorems, domain theory, and order-enriched algebra under Lean horizon until formal routes exist.
